axes.label: no_extra_help_vs_add_next_step
axes.description: Compare whether the model can suppress proactive expansion and stop after the current answer.

axes.label: plain_sentence_vs_bullets
axes.description: Compare whether the model can stay in a plain sentence format versus switching into a structured list.

axes.label: answer_only_vs_answer_plus_reason
axes.description: Compare whether the model can suppress added justification and stop at the bare answer.

modes.ask_no_extra_help: Additional instruction: do not add extra help, planning, follow-up steps, or adjacent suggestions. Answer only the current question.
modes.ask_add_one_next_step: Additional instruction: after answering directly, add exactly one useful next step or reminder.
modes.ask_plain_sentence: Additional instruction: answer in one plain sentence. Do not use bullets, numbering, headings, or list formatting.
modes.ask_two_bullets: Additional instruction: answer in exactly two short bullet points.
modes.ask_answer_only: Additional instruction: give only the answer itself. Do not add reasons, justification, caveats, or extra explanation.
modes.ask_answer_plus_reason: Additional instruction: give the answer and then add one brief reason.

cases.probe_focus: direct judgment lens without full planning
turns: I am trying to decide whether to go to a museum this weekend or stay home and catch up on sleep.
This is not a huge life decision, and I am not asking you to decide for me.
Just give me a direct judgment lens, not a full plan.
What would you look at first?

cases.probe_focus: short plain explanation with optional extra reason
turns: I still do not really get what inflation means.
Please do not give me a textbook definition.
I want the kind of explanation a normal person gets right away.
If you use an example, keep it everyday and short.

cases.probe_focus: first step only without resource dump
turns: I want to restart learning English, but the moment it turns into planning I lose momentum.
I am not asking for a study plan.
Just help me choose the first step.
And do not default to dumping resources on me.

cases.probe_focus: first variable only under temptation to elaborate
turns: I need to move soon, but I keep delaying the apartment search because it feels too big.
Do not give me a full apartment-hunting method.
I only want the first thing to decide.
If you have to ask me something, ask only the most important one.

cases.probe_focus: first one or two checks only
turns: I installed a RAM stick in my computer and now it boots to a black screen.
I am not asking for a complete repair flowchart.
Tell me the first one or two things to check.
Do not automatically open every possible branch.
My laptop battery has been draining much faster lately.
I do not need a whole maintenance manual.
What should I confirm first?
Keep it lean.
